edges.description: Endothelial dysfunction promotes a pro-remodeling pulmonary arterial environment.
Smooth muscle proliferation contributes to occlusive pulmonary vascular remodeling.
edges.target: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and elevated resistance define the pulmonary arterial hypertension phenotype.
edges.source: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Elevated pulmonary vascular resistance increases right ventricular afterload and can cause right ventricular failure.
edges.source: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Increased pulmonary vascular resistance limits cardiopulmonary reserve and manifests as dyspnea.
edges.source: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Severe pulmonary vascular resistance and reduced cardiac output can manifest as syncope.
edges.source: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Impaired cardiopulmonary output from PAH manifests as fatigue.
edges.source: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Right-sided heart strain and failure from PAH manifests as peripheral edema.
edges.source: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Serotonergic anorexigen signaling directly feeds pulmonary arterial smooth muscle growth.
edges.source: Serotonergic anorexigen smooth muscle growth signaling

edges.target: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

nodes.description: Removal of the implicated exposure is central when feasible; reversibility varies by agent and disease severity.

evidence.snippet: Dasatinib, a dual Src/Abl kinase inhibitor, used in the treatment of chronic myelogenous leukaemia was associated with cases of severe PAH, in part reversible after its withdrawal.

nodes.description: Culprit drugs or toxins can injure or dysregulate pulmonary arterial endothelium, creating a permissive vascular state for downstream smooth muscle growth and remodeling.

evidence.reference_title: Drug- and toxin-induced pulmonary arterial hypertension: Current state of the literature
evidence.snippet: Drug- and toxin-induced pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) is an increasingly important sub-group of group 1 pulmonary hypertension (PH).
Recently several studies raised the potential endothelial dysfunction that could be induced by interferon, and few cases of PAH have been reported with interferon therapy.

nodes.description: Exertional dyspnea is a common presenting symptom of pulmonary arterial hypertension.
Fatigue reflects reduced exercise capacity and cardiopulmonary reserve in pulmonary arterial hypertension.

evidence.reference_title: Treatment algorithm for pulmonary arterial hypertension
evidence.snippet: Pulmonary arterial hypertension leads to significant impairment in haemodynamics, right heart function, exercise capacity, quality of life and survival.

nodes.description: Peripheral edema can occur with right-heart congestion in advanced pulmonary arterial hypertension.
Pre-capillary PAH is the defining hemodynamic phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: State of the Art in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension: Molecular Basis, Imaging Modalities, and Right Heart Failure Treatment
evidence.snippet: Pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) is a specific subset of PH characterized by a normal pulmonary arterial wedge pressure (PAWP), combined with elevated mPAP and increased pulmonary vascular resistance (PVR)

nodes.description: Exposure-associated signaling can increase pulmonary arterial smooth muscle cell growth, narrowing the pulmonary vascular bed and feeding progressive vascular remodeling.

biological_processes: positive regulation of smooth muscle cell proliferation

evidence.snippet: The supposed mechanism is an increase in serotonin levels, which was demonstrated to act as a growth factor for the pulmonary arterial smooth muscle cells.

nodes.description: Progressive pulmonary microvascular obliteration and remodeling increase pulmonary vascular resistance, elevating right ventricular afterload and driving PAH morbidity.
nodes.id: Pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

evidence.snippet: Pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) is a rare disorder characterized by progressive obliteration of the pulmonary microvasculature, resulting in elevated pulmonary vascular resistance and premature death.

nodes.description: Elevated pulmonary vascular resistance increases right ventricular afterload and can progress to right-sided heart failure.

evidence.reference_title: State of the Art in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension: Molecular Basis, Imaging Modalities, and Right Heart Failure Treatment
evidence.snippet: Dysregulation of these pathways leads to a progressive vasculopathy marked by vasoconstriction, vascular proliferation, elevated right heart afterload, and ultimately right-sided heart failure.

nodes.description: Persistent or higher-risk disease is treated using standard PAH pathway-based therapies, often in combination and escalated by risk.

evidence.reference_title: Treatment algorithm for pulmonary arterial hypertension
evidence.snippet: Current therapies have mechanisms of action involving signallingviaone of four pathways: endothelin-1, nitric oxide, prostacyclin and bone morphogenetic protein/activin signalling.

evidence.reference_title: Treatment algorithm for pulmonary arterial hypertension
evidence.snippet: Efficacy has generally been greater with therapeutic combinations and with parenteral therapy compared with monotherapy or nonparenteral therapies, and maximal medical therapy is now four-drug therapy.

nodes.description: Appetite suppressant drugs such as aminorex, fenfluramine derivatives, and benfluorex are linked to PAH; serotonin-mediated pulmonary arterial smooth muscle growth is a proposed mechanism.
nodes.id: Serotonergic anorexigen smooth muscle growth signaling

biological_processes: positive regulation of smooth muscle cell proliferation

evidence.snippet: appetite suppressant drugs, such as aminorex, fenfluramine derivatives and benfluorex. These drugs have been confirmed to be risk factors for PAH and were withdrawn from the market.
The supposed mechanism is an increase in serotonin levels, which was demonstrated to act as a growth factor for the pulmonary arterial smooth muscle cells.

nodes.description: Syncope can occur in advanced PAH when cardiac output cannot meet demand during exertion or hemodynamic stress.
